'''Jared Waerea-Hargreaves'''  (born 20 January 1989) is a former New Zealand professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]] and [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at international level.<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=The Daily Telegraph|url=http://www.news.com.au/dailytelegraph/sport/nrl/player/0,26796,109834-108,00.html|title=League central:Jared Waerea-Hargreaves|access-date=3 July 2009}}</ref>

Waerea-Hargreaves started his NRL career at the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]]. He was recruited by the [[Sydney Roosters]], in 2010 and played in that year's [[2010 NRL Grand Final|Grand Final]] loss to St. George-Illawarra. He was a member of the [[Sydney Roosters]] winning Grand Final sides in [[2013 NRL Premiership|2013]],<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org" /> [[2018 NRL Grand Final|2018]] and [[2019 NRL Grand Final|2019]]. He has also played for the [[NRL All Stars team|NRL All Stars]].

==Early years==
Waerea-Hargreaves was born in [[Rotorua, New Zealand]], and is of [[Māori people|Māori]] and English descent.{{cn|date=May 2025}} He moved to [[Brisbane, Queensland]] with his mother when he was nine-years-old.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://thebeast.com.au/people/jared-waerea-hargreaves-the-family-man/|title=Jared Waerea-Hargreaves – The Family Man|date=29 February 2016|website=The Beast}}</ref>

Waerea-Hargreaves first made a name for himself playing schoolboy [[rugby union]] at [[Iona College (Queensland)]], through some impressive performances in the AIC competition, making the AIC rep side which played the GPS rep side. Waerea-Hargreaves joined the [[NSW Waratahs|Waratahs Academy]] in Sydney. Waerea-Hargreaves played for [[Australia national rugby union team|Australia]] at the [[2007 Under 19 Rugby World Championship]] in Ireland alongside future-[[Super Rugby]] players, such as [[Dan Palmer (rugby)|Dan Palmer]], [[Pat McCabe (rugby union)|Pat McCabe]] and [[Blair Connor]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/roosters-star-jared-waereahargreaves-wont-rule-out-return-to-rugby-union-20131123-2y2j6.html|title=Roosters star Jared Waerea-Hargreaves won't rule out return to rugby union|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=23 November 2013|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/worldly-waereahargreaves-journey-finds-benevolence-in-the-violence-20101010-16dz2.html|title=Worldly Waerea-Hargreaves journey finds benevolence in the violence|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=10 October 2010|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref>

==Club career==
===Manly Warringah Sea Eagles===
[[File:Jared Waerea-Hargreaves 2008.jpg|thumb|right|Waerea-Hargreaves playing for the Manly Sea Eagles in 2008]]
Waerea-Hargreaves was signed by the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] recruitment manager, [[Noel Cleal]], despite never having played rugby league.<ref>{{cite news|last=Massoud|first=Josh|publisher=Fox Sports News (Australia)|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,24504713-23214,00.html|title=Soldiering on for his long-lost dad|date=16 October 2008|access-date=3 July 2009}}</ref>

Waerea-Hargreaves played in the Sea Eagles [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team in 2008 and 2009.

Waerea-Hargreaves made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut against the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in round 9, playing off the interchange bench in the Sea Eagles 22–20 win at [[Suncorp Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/manly-sea-eagles-beat-brisbane-broncos-22-20/story-e6frexni-1225710519207|title=Sea Eagles Beat Broncos 22-20|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref> In his next match, in round 15, Waerea-Hargreaves scored his first NRL career try in the Sea Eagles 20–14 victory over the [[Canberra Raiders]] at [[Brookvale Oval]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/articles/2009/06/21/1245522728323.html?page=fullpage|title=Sea Eagles all smiles after shaking off Raiders|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=22 June 2009|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref> Waerea-Hargreaves played 6 matches and scored a try during the [[2009 NRL season|season]]. He was also named in the [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|Toyota Cup]] team of the year.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/holden-cup/eight-clubs-represented-in-toyota-cup-team-of-the-year/story-e6frf3qu-1225768330928|title=Eight Clubs Represented in Toyota Cup Team of the Year|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref>
 
===Sydney Roosters===
====2010====
Waerea-Hargreaves joined the [[Sydney Roosters]] in 2010 on a three-year deal.  In week 1 of the finals, against [[Wests Tigers]], Waerea-Hargreaves was on a receiving end of big hit from [[Simon Dwyer]]. The Roosters won 19–15 in golden point extra time due to a [[Shaun Kenny-Dowall]] 70-metre intercept try.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/shaun-kenny-dowall-scores-in-golden-point-as-roosters-pip-wests-tigers/story-fn2mcuj6-1225924531327|title=Shaun Keeny Dowall Scores in Golden Point as Roosters Pip Wests Tigers|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref> Waerea-Hargreaves played off the interchange bench in the [[2010 NRL Grand Final]], losing 32–8 to the [[St George-Illawarra Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/roosters-lead-the-dragons-8-6-at-halftime-in-the-nrl-grand-final/story-e6frexnr-1225933487068|title=Roosters Lead the Dragons 8-6 at half-time in NRL Grand Final|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref> He played in 19 matches for the Roosters in [[2010 NRL season|2010]].

====2011====
In round 25, against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], Waerea-Hargreaves scored his first club try for the Roosters in the 36–25 win at the [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|SFS]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/sydney-roosters-storm-home-to-pull-in-cronulla/story-e6frg7mf-1226118819327|title=Roosters Storm Home to Pull in Cronulla|newspaper=The Australian|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref> He played in 21 matches and scored a try for the [[2011 NRL season|season]].

====2012====
[[File:JARED WAEREA-HARGREAVES (6913423263).jpg|thumb|right|Waerea-Hargreaves playing for the Sydney Roosters in 2012]]

On 6 February, Waerea-Hargreaves played for the [[NRL All Stars team|NRL All Stars]] on the interchange bench in the 28–12 win over the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/indigenous-all-stars-take-on-nrl-all-stars-for-arthur-beetson-trophy-in-clash-at-skilled-park/story-e6frf3ou-1226262734384|title=Indigenous all Stars Take on NRL All Stars for Arthur Beetson Trophy|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref>

He played in 23 matches and scored 3 tries in [[2012 NRL season|2012]].

====2013====
In round 9, he copped a 5-week suspension after being sent off for a head tackle against the Sea Eagles.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/sydney-roosters-move-to-second-on-nrl-ladder-with-16-4-win-over-manly-at-brookvale-oval/story-fn2mcuj6-1226641516859|title=Roosters Move to Second on NRL Ladder|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref> Waerea-Hargreaves played prop in his second [[2013 NRL Grand Final|grand final]] against the Sea Eagles, the Roosters winning 26–18.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/rolling-coverage-of-sydney-roosters-v-manly-sea-eagles-nrl-grand-final/story-fni3fbgz-1226733760880|title=Rolling Coverage of Roosters v Sea Eagles|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/nrl-grand-final-player-ratings/tabid/10874/newsid/74996/default.aspx|title=NRL Grand Final player ratings|work=NRL.com|access-date=2015-11-01|author=Dominic Brock|date=6 October 2013 }}</ref> Waerea-Hargreaves played in 21 matches and scored a try in the Roosters' successful [[2013 NRL season|season]]. 

====2014====
In round 11, against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] at [[ANZ Stadium]], Waerea-Hargreaves played his 100th NRL career match in the Roosters 32–12 win.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/sonny-bill-williams-inflicts-some-pain-on-his-old-club-as-the-roosters-beat-the-bulldogs-3212/story-fni3fbgz-1226929015706|title=Sonny Bill Williams Inflicts Some Pain on his Old Club|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref> In round 19, against the [[Penrith Panthers]] at the SFS, he played his 100th match for the Roosters in the 32–12 win.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/reigning-premiers-sydney-roosters-put-penrith-panthers-down-with-a-gritty-3212-victory/story-fni3gn3s-1226994622403|title=Roosters Put Panthers Down with a Gritty 32-12 Victory|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref> Waerea-Hargreaves finished off the [[2014 NRL season|2014 season]] with him playing in 26 matches and scoring a try. In December, the [[Canberra Raiders]] offered Waerea-Hargreaves a multimillion-dollar contract but ended negotiations after he failed to meet a deadline.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/sydney-roosters-prop-jared-waereahargreaves-in-the-sights-of-canberra-raiders-20141207-1223ta.html|title=Sydney Roosters prop Jared Waerea-Hargreaves in the sights of Canberra Raiders|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=2015-11-01|author=Chris Wilson|date=7 December 2014 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.canberratimes.com.au/rugby-league/canberra-raiders/canberra-raiders-end-negotiations-with-sydney-roosters-prop-jared-waereahargreaves-20141214-126zsy.html|title=Canberra Raiders end negotiations with Sydney Roosters prop Jared Waerea-Hargreaves|work=The Canberra Times|access-date=2015-11-01|author=Lee Gaskin|date=14 December 2014 }}</ref>

====2015====
On 24 January, Waerea-Hargreaves was the co-captain of Roosters [[2015 Auckland Nines]] squad alongside [[Mitchell Pearce]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/roosters-2015-nines-squad/|title=ROOSTERS NAME 2015 NINES SQUAD|work=Rugby League Week|access-date=2015-11-01|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160203055113/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/roosters-2015-nines-squad/|archive-date=3 February 2016|url-status=dead}}</ref> On 13 February, he played for the [[NRL All Stars team|NRL All Stars]] in the [[2015 All Stars match]]. The NRL All Stars lost 20–6 to the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/the-indigenous-all-stars-have-beaten-the-nrl-stars-20-6-at-cbus-stadium/story-fni3fbgz-1227219053165|title=Indigenous All Stars Have Beaten the NRL Stars 20-6|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref> In round 23, against the [[Parramatta Eels]], Waerea-Hargreaves scored one of the best solo tries of the season, scoring a crucial try by steamrolling two defenders and brushed off two more to score next to the posts in the Roosters 28–18 win.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/sydney-roosters/dadtobe-jared-waereahargreaves-produces-crucial-play-in-sydney-roosters-win-over-parramatta-eels-20150815-gizxnz.html|title=New dad Jared Waerea-Hargreaves produces crucial play in Sydney Roosters win over Parramatta Eels|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=15 August 2015 |access-date=26 February 2016}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2015-sydney-roosters-come-from-behind-to-beat-parramatta-eels-scores-updates-live-blog/story-fn2mcuj6-1227483878667|title=Sydney Roosters Come From Behind to Beat Parramatta Eels|date=26 February 2016|work=Fox Sports|access-date=26 February 2016|author=James Phelps}}</ref> In the next match against the [[Brisbane Broncos]], his season ended after he suffered a knee injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/roosters-v-broncos-jared-waerea-hargreaves-out-for-season-as-roosters-win-thriller-over-broncos/story-fn2mcuj6-1227491967857|title=Jared Warea-Hargreaves out for season as Roosters win thriller|date=26 February 2016|work=Fox Sports|access-date=26 February 2016|author=James Hooper}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/mitchell-pearce-jared-waereahargreaves-suffer-potential-seasonending-injuries-as-sydney-roosters-down-brisbane-broncos-20150822-gj5hql.html|title=Mitchell Pearce, Jared Waerea-Hargreaves suffer potential season-ending injuries as Sydney Roosters down Brisbane Broncos|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=22 August 2015 |access-date=26 February 2016}}</ref> Waerea-Hargreaves finished the [[2015 NRL season|year]] with him playing in 19 matches and scoring 2 tries.

====2016====
In round 9, against the [[Newcastle Knights]], Waerea-Hargreaves made his return for the Roosters from injury, playing off the interchange bench in the 38–0 win at the Sydney Football Stadium.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/79339542/jared-waereahargreaves-mitchell-pearce-return-for-sydney-roosters|title=Jared Waerea-Hargreaves, Mitchell Pearce return for Sydney Roosters|website=Stuff.co.nz|date=2016-04-27|access-date=2016-10-03}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-2016-mitchell-pearce-leads-sydney-roosters-to-massive-win-over-newcastle-knights/news-story/2e429e508b81710400692d53fe88dad7|title=Mitchell Pearce Leads Sydney Roosters to Massive Win Over Newcastle Knights|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2016-10-03}}</ref> Waerea-Hargreaves finished the [[2016 NRL season]] with him playing in 14 matches for the Roosters. 
====2017====
Waerea-Hargreaves played in every game of the [[2017 NRL season]] for the Roosters as the club fell one game short of a grand final appearance suffering a shock defeat by North Queensland in the preliminary final 29–16.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/sep/23/cowboys-fairytale-continues-into-grand-final-with-upset-win-over-roosters | title=Cowboys' fairytale continues into grand final with upset win over Roosters | website=[[TheGuardian.com]] | date=23 September 2017 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web | url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTEtMTY5Ni00LS0tLS0tMTM4NzYtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t | title=Custom Match List - Rugby League Project }}</ref>

====2018====
Waerea-Hargreaves was part of the Easts side which won their 4th minor premiership in 6 seasons. On 30 September, Waerea-Hargreaves played in Eastern Suburbs 21–6 victory over Melbourne in the [[2018 NRL grand final]] winning his second premiership as a player.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=12134658 |title=Sydney Roosters win NRL grand final 21-6 over Melbourne - NZ Herald |website=www.nzherald.co.nz |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181002141545/https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=12134658 |archive-date=2018-10-02}} </ref>

====2019====
In round 17 against North Queensland, Waerea-Hargreaves was placed on report in the club's 15–12 loss at the [[Central Coast Stadium]] after allegedly headbutting North Queensland player [[Josh McGuire]].  Waerea-Hargreaves was later cleared of any wrongdoing but was initially facing a two match ban.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/07/15/cotric-facing-four-week-ban-over-dangerous-throw-charge/|title=JWH not guilty; Cotric to miss three games|website=NRL|date=16 July 2019 }}</ref>
In round 25 against arch rivals [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]], Waerea-Hargreaves was placed on report after hitting Souths player [[Liam Knight]] with a late tackle around the head which left Knight bleeding on the ground.  The Roosters would go on to lose the match 16–10 at [[ANZ Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/its-a-joke-legend-steve-roach-takes-aim-at-nrl-over-sam-burgess-jared-waereahargreaves-charges/news-story/301d11eceb6a423824470d63f517e294/|title=Legend Steve Roach takes aim at NRL over Sam Burgess, Jared Waerea-Hargreaves charges|website=Fox Sports|date=6 September 2019 }}</ref>

In the qualifying final against South Sydney, Waerea-Hargreaves was placed on report and later suspended for one match after being found guilty of tripping Souths player [[James Roberts (rugby league)|James Roberts]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/09/17/waerea-hargreaves-guilty-of-tripping-banned-for-one-game/|title=JWH guilty of tripping, banned for preliminary final|website=NRL|date=17 September 2019 }}</ref>
Waerea-Hargreaves missed the club's preliminary final victory over Melbourne but played in the [[2019 NRL Grand Final]] where the [[Sydney Roosters]] won the match 14–8 against the [[Canberra Raiders]] at [[ANZ Stadium]].  It was the club's second consecutive premiership and the third premiership for Waerea-Hargreaves as a player.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49950757|title=Sydney Roosters beat Canberra Raiders to win NRL Grand Final|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

On 22 November 2019, it was announced that Waerea-Hargreaves had signed a three-year contract extension to keep him at the club until the end of the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/11/22/roosters-re-sign-waerea-hargreaves-through-to-2023/|title=Roosters re-sign Waerea-Hargreaves through to 2023|website=NRL|date=22 November 2019 }}</ref>

====2020====
Waerea-Hargreaves played 18 games for the club in the [[2020 NRL season]].  They fell short of a third successive premiership losing to Canberra in the elimination final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/10/09/raiders-march-on-as-three-peat-dream-ends-for-roosters/|title='More than a team': Ricky's resilient Raiders keep the dream alive|website=www.nrl.com|date=9 October 2020 }}</ref>

====2021====
Waerea-Hargreaves played a total of 23 games for the Sydney Roosters in the [[2021 NRL season]] including the club's two finals matches.  The Sydney Roosters would be eliminated from the second week of the finals losing to Manly 42-6.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/09/16/they-dont-rate-us-how-injury-toll-eased-pressure-for-revitalised-roosters/|title='They don't rate us': How injury toll eased pressure for revitalised Roosters|website=www.nrl.com|date=16 September 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-09-17/nrl-manly-sea-eagles-defeat-sydney-roosters-in-semi-final/100472900/|title=Manly Sea Eagles hammer Sydney Roosters 42-6 to advance to NRL preliminary finals|newspaper=ABC News|date=17 September 2021}}</ref>

====2022====
In round 24 of the [[2022 NRL season]], Waerea-Hargreaves was sent to the sin bin for a dangerous high tackle during the Sydney Roosters upset victory over [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2022-08-26/nrl-live-score-updates-panthers-warriors-storm-roosters/101371276/|title=Roosters outlast Storm in thrilling finals preview, Penrith tune up for finals with Warriors belting|website=www.abc.net.au|date=26 August 2022 }}</ref>
In the elimination final, Waerea-Hargreaves was sent to the sin bin for a dangerous tackle in the club's upset defeat to arch-rivals South Sydney which ended their season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-sin-bin-punch-sydney-roosters-victor-radley-south-sydney-rabbitohs-taane-milne-thomas-burgess-ashley-klein-video-high-tackle/news-story/769cf6e583f605ae656c0617f5064469/|title=SEVEN players binned in historic first after Roosters-Rabbitohs clash explodes|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=13 September 2022 }}</ref>
In the third group stage match at the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]], Waerea-Hargreaves was sent to the sin bin for a stiff arm to the head of Dan Norman during New Zealand's 48-10 victory over the Republic of Ireland.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63434684#:~:text=New%20Zealand%20cruised%20to%20a,Jamaica%20against%20Lebanon%20on%20Sunday./|title=Rugby League World Cup: Crushing New Zealand win leaves Ireland facing exit|website=www.bbc.co.uk|date=28 October 2022 }}</ref>

==== 2023 ====
Waerea-Hargreaves signed a one-year extension with the club for the 2024 season. In round 26 of the [[2023 NRL season]], Waerea-Hargreaves was placed on report for a high tackle on Wests Tigers player [[Apisai Koroisau]]. Waerea-Hargreaves was later suspended for seven matches, which ended his season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/live-nrl-axed-rooster-returns-for-mustwin-clash-as-tigers-fight-to-avoid-wooden-spoon/news-story/8ff0988b55b4dde31a12d48bb1ede88f/|title=Star's sizzling return amid Roosters' injury blow as damning stat exposes Tigers: Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=26 August 2023 }}</ref>

==== 2024 ====

On 20 February 2024, Waerea-Hargreaves announced he would depart the Sydney Roosters following the season's conclusion.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-02-20 |title=Jared Waerea-Hargreaves to Play His Final Season with the Roosters |url=https://www.roosters.com.au/news/2024/02/20/jared-waerea-hargreaves-to-play-his-final-season-with-the-roosters/ |access-date=2024-05-12 |website=Sydney Roosters |language=en}}</ref>

In round 10 of the [[2024 NRL season]], Waerea-Hargreaves played his 300th game for the Sydney Roosters in their 38-18 victory over the New Zealand Warriors.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-round-10-live-sydney-roosters-v-warriors-at-allianz-stadium-20240512-p5jcwv.html/|title=Doubles from Young, Crichton help Roosters dominate Warriors|website=www.smh.com.au|date=12 May 2024 }}</ref> Waerea-Hargreaves would go outright first for most games played for the club during their round 18 match against St. George Illawarra. Waerea-Hargreaves was sin binned during the game. Waerea-Hargreaves played 18 games for the Sydney Roosters in his final season at the club including their preliminary final defeat against Melbourne.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-07-04 |title='Equal with those guys': Ultimate praise as 'true warrior' JWH celebrated ahead of Roosters record |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/roosters/nrl-2024-jared-waereahargreaves-307th-nrl-game-sydney-roosters-record-alltime-best-props/news-story/a004dbbb608b23fbb84f1f8d4eeefcbf |access-date=2024-07-05 |website=Fox Sports |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2024-jared-waereahargreaves-sin-bin-what-happened-milestone-high-tackle-roosters-v-dragons-rugby-league-news/news-story/370cbed1885f98e03d0b4158e7ee66b7/|title='That's not 10 Ashley': JWH sin binned in milestone game to earn mammoth ban|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=8 July 2024 }}</ref>

=== Hull Kingston Rovers ===

A day after announcing his departure from Sydney Roosters, he had signed for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] effective for 2025.<ref>{{cite web|website=Sky Sports|date=21 Feb 2024|accessdate=3 Oct 2024|title=Jared Waerea-Hargreaves: Hull KR swoop to sign NRL star and New Zealand international for 2025|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12217/13076313/jared-waerea-hargreaves-hull-kr-swoop-to-sign-nrl-star-and-new-zealand-international-for-2025}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Jared Waerea-Hargreaves: Hull KR swoop to sign NRL star and New Zealand international for 2025 |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12217/13076313/jared-waerea-hargreaves-hull-kr-swoop-to-sign-nrl-star-and-new-zealand-international-for-2025 |access-date=2024-05-12 |website=Sky Sports |language=en}}</ref> In April, Waerea-Hargreaves announced he would retire following the [[2025 Hull Kingston Rovers season|2025 season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12217/13348946/jared-waerea-hargreaves-hull-kr-prop-to-retire-at-end-of-super-league-season|title=Jared Waerea-Hargreaves: Hull KR prop to retire at end of Super League season|publisher=Sky Sports|access-date=14 April 2025}}</ref>

On 7 June, he played in Hull Kingston Rovers 8-6 [[2025 Challenge Cup final]] victory over Warrington Wolves, winning Hull KR's first major trophy in 40 years.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref> 

On 18 September, Waerea-Hargeaves played in Hull Kingston Rovers final game of the regular season to claim the League Leaders Shield in victory over Warrington <ref>https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c39rzxxpyjeo</ref>

Waerea-Hargreaves ended his career as [[treble (rugby league)|treble]] winners with Hull Kingston Rovers after defeating Wigan Warriors 24-6 in the [[2025 Super League Grand Final]] to claim club's first league title in 40 years and first of the Super League era.<ref name="Freeman">{{cite news |last1=Freeman |first1=Jay |title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final for historic treble |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo |access-date=11 October 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=11 October 2025}}</ref>

==International career==

He was selected as part of the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]] [[2009 Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations]] squad and made his debut against [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]].<ref name="NZ_Herald_10602715">{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10602715&ref=rss|title=League: Chance for rookie Kiwis|date=12 October 2009|agency=[[NZPA]]|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|access-date=6 October 2011}}</ref> Waerea-Hargreaves scored two tries against [[France national rugby league team|France]] in the Kiwis 62–12 win at [[Stade Ernest Wallon]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/news/lhqnews/perrett-treble-sinks-france/2009/11/01/1257010093686.html|title=Perrett treble sinks France|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=November 2009|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref>

He was selected to play for New Zealand from the interchange bench in the [[2010 ANZAC Test|ANZAC Test]], in their 12–8 loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/kangaroos-v-new-zealand-trans-tasman-test-at-aami-park-in-melbourne/story-e6frep5x-1225863712628|title=Kangaroos v New Zealand Test at AAMI Park|publisher=Couriermail.com.au|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref>

Waerea-Hargreaves played a match for New Zealand in the [[2010 Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations]], a 76–12 victory over [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/new-zealand-beat-papua-new-guinea-76-12-in-four-nations/story-e6frexnr-1225945564820|title=NZ Beat Papua New Guinea 76-12|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref>

Waerea-Hargreaves was selected in the New Zealand [[2011 Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations]] squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/kangaroos/nine-players-from-2011-nrl-grand-final-named-in-new-zealands-squad-for-upcoming-four-nations/story-fn2mcuhw-1226158248831|title=Nine Players from 2011 NRL Grand Final Named in New Zealand's Squad|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref>

Waerea-Hargreaves played off the interchange bench in the October test against Australia.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/kangaroos/australia-grind-it-out-in-second-half-to-win-18-10-over-new-zealand-in-townsville-test-match/story-fn2mcuhw-1226495026875|title=Australia Grind It Out in Second Half to Win 18-10 over New Zealand in Townsville Test Match|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref>

[[File:Jared Waerea-Hargreaves at the Arboretum.jpg|thumb|right|Waerea-Hargreaves on media duty for the Kiwis ahead of the 2013 ANZAC Test]]

For the [[2013 ANZAC Test]], Waerea-Hargreaves played for New Zealand at prop in their 32–12 loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/australia-beats-new-zealand-32-12-at-canbera-stadium/story-e6frexnr-1226624627487|title=Australia Beat New Zealand 32-12|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref> 

[[File:Jared Waerea-Hargreaves.JPG|thumb|right|Waerea-Hargreaves playing for the Kiwis at the 2013 RLWC]]
He played for the Kiwis in all 7 matches of the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]] including the 34–2 loss to Australia in the final.<ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/25168674 | title=New Zealand 2-34 Australia | work=BBC Sport }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/world-cup-final-player-ratings/tabid/10874/newsid/75602/default.aspx|title=World Cup final player ratings|work=NRL.com|date=30 November 2013 |access-date=2015-11-01}}</ref>

On 6 September 2016, Waerea-Hargreaves was added to the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]] train-on squad for the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/warriors-dominate-kiwis-train-on-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/101077/default.aspx|title=Warriors dominate Kiwis train-on squad|website=NRL.com|date=6 September 2016 |access-date=2016-10-03}}</ref>

== Post playing ==
In September 2025, it was reported at Hargreaves would return to the Roosters in a mentor role following his retirement from rugby league.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-09-24 |title=Premiership hero set for return to Roosters as Bulldogs sign Dolphins playmaker |url=https://au.news.yahoo.com/roosters-premiership-hero-set-for-return-to-club-as-bulldogs-sign-playmaker-from-dolphins-031222589.html |access-date=2025-09-24 |website=Yahoo News |language=en-AU}}</ref>
